store.tagline: Per-tenant end-to-end encrypted file storage: AEAD-sealed chunks in any cloud bucket, the index and keys inside an attested TDX enclave, and grants a tenant can verify and revoke.
store.keywords: drive, storage, files, encrypted, e2ee, xchacha20, merkle, gdpr export, share, appgrant, ra-tls, attestation, enclave os, tdx, confidential computing, mcp
store.description: Privasys Drive is the per-tenant encrypted file store behind Privasys Wallet, Chat and Secure Room, and a storage backend any platform app or agent can be granted scoped access to. File content is split into content-addressed chunks, each AEAD-sealed (XChaCha20-Poly1305) under a per-file key wrapped under the tenant's key; the cloud bucket only ever sees opaque ciphertext. The directory index, ACLs and wrapped keys live inside the attested enclave on the sealed per-app volume. Every file carries a Merkle root so clients detect tampering, and a GDPR-grade ZIP export (plaintext or ciphertext) is built in. An instance runs in one of two attestable operating modes: sovereign (only tenants can unlock their data; the operator holds no key) or escrowed (tenant keys carry an escrow wrap under the operator's master key, unlockable only through an audited, policy-gated recovery path).

configure.name: configure
configure.title: Drive instance settings
configure.description: Set the instance operating mode and defaults, lifting the configure-then-freeze gate. The mode is immutable once set and is part of the attested configuration: 'sovereign' means only tenants can ever unlock their data (the operator holds no key); 'escrowed' adds an org master-key escrow with policy-gated recovery. Settings persist on the sealed volume and re-apply automatically on restart.

mode.description: Operating mode, immutable once set. sovereign: tenant keys have the tenant as the only owner; no operator unlock path exists. escrowed: every tenant key additionally carries an escrow wrap under the org master key (MEK_org), and every escrow is disclosed to the tenant via the audit log. Escrowed setup (org_mek_ref + recovery policy) is sent via the API/CLI, not this form. The audited recover_tenant enforcement gate ships next; escrow-wrap and disclosure are active now.

x-privasys.label: Operating mode
x-privasys.help: sovereign: only tenants can unlock their data; the operator holds no key. escrowed: adds org escrow (MEK_org) with policy-gated, disclosed recovery; set org_mek_ref + recovery via the API/CLI.

quota_default_bytes.description: Default per-tenant storage quota in bytes. 0 means unlimited.

mgmt_base_url.description: Control-plane API base URL (e.g. https://api.developer.privasys.org). When set, the instance refreshes stale vault attestation tokens itself via its manager-minted app identity, so file operations self-heal after idle instead of returning 409 vault_key_stale until the owner re-arms. Mutable.

embeddings_base_url.description: OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint base URL on the confidential-AI fleet, tried first for semantic indexing; the local CPU embedder is the fallback. Mutable.

embeddings_model.description: Embedding model name on the endpoint (must produce 1024-dimensional vectors).

chat_model.description: Fleet chat model for conversation digests and summaries (same host + pin as embeddings). Empty disables Drive-side generation.

object_backend.description: Instance object store for encrypted chunk bodies (a backend only ever sees opaque AEAD ciphertext). local = the sealed /data volume (default). gcs/s3/ovh require object_bucket + object_credential. A tenant may still bring its own bucket on top of this default.

object_bucket.description: Bucket name for the gcs/s3/ovh object backend.

object_credential.description: Secret access material for the object backend: a GCS service-account JSON for gcs, or an s3-keypair JSON ({access_key_id,secret_access_key,region,endpoint}) for s3/ovh. Sealed on the instance; never disclosed.

embeddings_dependency.description: Attested-dependency pin for the fleet: the canonical dependency-set JSON from the app record ({"entries":[...]}, as set via the platform's apps dependencies API). When present, every embeddings call dials RA-TLS and refuses any peer that does not match the pinned measurements and required OIDs (fail closed). Leave empty only for off-platform endpoints.

embeddings_allow_debug.description: Permit dev-profile fleet images on the pinned dial. Development environments only.

assistant_enclave_token.description: RAG-in-enclave (§8.7): shared secret the confidential-AI enclave presents (Authorization: Assistant <token>) to call Drive's read-only, AI-scoped RAG tools on behalf of a user. Interim gate, later replaced by inbound mutual RA-TLS. Empty disables the path (fail closed).

assistant_enclave_measurement.description: RAG-in-enclave (§8.7): the confidential-AI workload identity the final mutual-RA-TLS gate will require of the caller. Recorded for disclosure; not yet enforced.

tools.name: configure
tools.title: Drive instance settings
tools.description: Set the instance operating mode and defaults, lifting the configure-then-freeze gate. The mode is immutable once set and is part of the attested configuration. Returns {status: "configured", mode}.

mode.description: Operating mode, immutable once set. sovereign: tenant keys have the tenant as the only owner; no operator unlock path exists. escrowed: adds an org master-key escrow with policy-gated recovery (not yet available in this build).

x-privasys.label: Operating mode
x-privasys.help: sovereign: only tenants can unlock their data; the operator holds no key. escrowed: adds org escrow with policy-gated recovery.

quota_default_bytes.description: Default per-tenant storage quota in bytes. 0 means unlimited.

mgmt_base_url.description: Control-plane API base URL. When set, the instance refreshes stale vault attestation tokens itself via its manager-minted app identity (self-heal for 409 vault_key_stale). Mutable.

embeddings_base_url.description: OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint base URL on the confidential-AI fleet, tried first for semantic indexing; the local CPU embedder is the fallback. Mutable.

embeddings_model.description: Embedding model name on the endpoint (must produce 1024-dimensional vectors).

chat_model.description: Fleet chat model for conversation digests and summaries (same host + pin as embeddings). Empty disables Drive-side generation.

embeddings_dependency.description: Attested-dependency pin for the fleet: the canonical dependency-set JSON from the app record ({"entries":[...]}, as set via the platform's apps dependencies API). When present, every embeddings call dials RA-TLS and refuses any peer that does not match the pinned measurements and required OIDs (fail closed). Leave empty only for off-platform endpoints.

embeddings_allow_debug.description: Permit dev-profile fleet images on the pinned dial. Development environments only.

assistant_enclave_token.description: RAG-in-enclave (§8.7): shared secret the confidential-AI enclave presents (Authorization: Assistant <token>) to call Drive's read-only, AI-scoped RAG tools on behalf of a user. Interim gate, later replaced by inbound mutual RA-TLS. Empty disables the path (fail closed).

assistant_enclave_measurement.description: RAG-in-enclave (§8.7): the confidential-AI workload identity the final mutual-RA-TLS gate will require of the caller. Recorded for disclosure; not yet enforced.

tools.name: status
tools.title: Instance status
tools.description: Report the instance status: state (awaiting_config | ready), operating mode, version and message.

tools.name: list_root
tools.title: List drive root
tools.description: List the root-level folders and files of a tenant's drive. Names and metadata come from the enclave-held index; content never leaves the enclave unencrypted except to an authorised caller.

tenant_id.description: The tenant whose drive root to list.

tools.name: list_folder
tools.title: List a folder
tools.description: List the immediate children (folders and files) of a folder.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the folder belongs to.

folder_id.description: The folder node id returned by list_root, list_folder or create_folder.

tools.name: create_folder
tools.title: Create a folder
tools.description: Create a folder, optionally under a parent folder (omit parent_id for the drive root). Returns the new node.

tenant_id.description: The tenant to create the folder in.

parent_id.description: Parent folder node id; omit for the drive root.

name.description: Folder name, unique within the parent.

tools.name: write_file
tools.title: Write a file
tools.description: Upload a file. The content is chunked, AEAD-sealed under a fresh per-file key and stored content-addressed; the returned node carries the Merkle root over the ciphertext chunks. For large files use the streaming REST endpoint instead (this tool caps content at 8 MiB).

tenant_id.description: The tenant to write into.

parent_id.description: Parent folder node id; omit for the drive root.

name.description: File name, unique within the parent.

mime.description: Optional MIME type hint, e.g. application/pdf.

index.description: Set false to exclude this file from the semantic index (files are searchable by default).

tools.name: read_file
tools.title: Read a file
tools.description: Read a file's plaintext (decrypted inside the enclave for this authorised call only) plus its metadata and Merkle root. Files over 8 MiB must use the streaming REST endpoint.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the file belongs to.

tools.name: delete_node
tools.title: Delete a file or folder
tools.description: Delete a file, or a folder recursively. Sealed chunk data for deleted files is removed from the object store.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the node belongs to.

tools.name: changes
tools.title: Change feed
tools.description: Read the tenant's change feed (create/delete events with actor attribution) strictly after a sequence number. Drives ingestion pipelines and sync clients.

tenant_id.description: The tenant whose feed to read.

since.description: Return changes with seq strictly greater than this. 0 (default) starts from the beginning.

tools.name: my_drive
tools.title: My drive
tools.description: Get or create the caller's personal drive (User tenant). Idempotent: the first call creates it, later calls return the same tenant. Returns the tenant_id the other tools take.

tools.name: share_link_create
tools.title: Create a share link
tools.description: Mint a share link on a file or folder (owner/admin). mode open grants on redemption; mode restricted requires the listed attributes and files an owner-approved access request. Returns the link id and the secret (shown exactly once).

tenant_id.description: Tenant the node belongs to.

mode.description: open: anyone with the link gets access on sign-in. restricted: visitors present attributes and the owner approves each request.

required_attributes.description: Restricted mode: canonical attribute keys the visitor must present (e.g. name, email).

tools.name: redeem_link
tools.title: Redeem a share link
tools.description: Redeem a share link as the caller. Open links grant access immediately; restricted links file an access request carrying the presented attributes (the sharer's wallet is notified; the attributes are never persisted on the drive). Returns granted or pending with the request id.

link_id.description: The link id from the share URL.

attributes.description: Restricted mode: the attributes the caller presents (all required keys must be non-empty).

tools.name: list_link_requests
tools.title: List access requests
tools.description: List a tenant's restricted-link access requests (owner/admin). Requests carry the requester's sub only; the presented attributes live in the sharer's wallet.

tenant_id.description: Tenant to list for.

tools.name: decide_link_request
tools.title: Decide an access request
tools.description: Approve or deny a pending access request (owner/admin). Approval mints the requester's grant; either way the requester's wallet is notified of the outcome.

tenant_id.description: Tenant the request belongs to.

tools.name: create_conversation
tools.title: Create a conversation
tools.description: Provision a chat conversation's Drive directory (under 'Chat conversations/') with an empty no_index transcript and a files/ folder. Idempotent per title+date. Returns conversation_id, transcript_id and files_folder_id.

tenant_id.description: Tenant to create the conversation in.

title.description: Conversation title (slugified into the directory name).

date.description: Optional YYYY-MM-DD prefix for the directory (the conversation start date).

tools.name: append_turn
tools.title: Append a conversation turn
tools.description: Append one serialised JSON turn to a conversation's transcript (conversation.jsonl). The transcript stays no_index; only the finalized digest is searchable.

turn.description: One turn serialised as a JSON object (the caller owns the schema).

tools.name: list_conversations
tools.title: List conversations
tools.description: List the caller's chat conversations with their transcript/digest ids and finalized flag.

tools.name: get_conversation
tools.title: Get a conversation
tools.description: Return a conversation's metadata and its full transcript text, for cross-device resume.

tools.name: delete_conversation
tools.title: Delete a conversation
tools.description: Permanently delete a conversation and everything in it: the transcript, its files/ attachments, and the finalized digest. The sealed blobs are reclaimed. Irreversible.

tools.name: attach_to_conversation
tools.title: Attach a file to a conversation
tools.description: Upload a file into a conversation's files/ folder. intent 'session' (default) stores it no_index and auto-expiring (use in this chat); intent 'knowledge' persists and indexes it (add to my knowledge base). Returns the node and the intent.

intent.description: session = use in this chat (no_index, auto-expire); knowledge = persist + index.

expires_unix.description: Optional expiry override for intent session (unix seconds).

tools.name: finalize_conversation
tools.title: Finalise a conversation
tools.description: Mark a conversation complete: Drive generates its digest.md via the pinned fleet, grounded on the provenance you accumulated (it may cite ONLY those chips, resolved and validated at write time; dead links dropped). Claims are marked extracted/inferred/ambiguous. Regenerates in place on re-finalise. Requires a configured chat model.

provenance.description: The citation chips actually used in the conversation.

section_id.description: Stable section anchor; omit for a whole-file citation.

tools.name: get_graph
tools.title: Get the knowledge graph
tools.description: Return the tenant's node/edge graph: files and folders (coloured by class — memory / conversation / document) with typed edges (citation, wikilink, containment). Access-scoped to the caller's tenant.

tools.name: enable_ai
tools.title: Enable a directory for AI
tools.description: Grant the assistant read access to a directory subtree ('Shared with: Assistant'). The assistant's scoped search then reaches it. Memory/ and Chat conversations/ are always in scope; everything else is opt-in.

node_id.description: The folder (or file) to enable for AI.

tools.name: disable_ai
tools.title: Disable a directory for AI
tools.description: Revoke the assistant's grant on a directory subtree.

tools.name: list_ai_scope
tools.title: List the AI scope
tools.description: List the directories the assistant may read: explicit grants plus the always-scoped defaults (Memory/, Chat conversations/).

tools.name: get_folder_tree
tools.title: Get a folder tree
tools.description: Return a folder's structure derived from the index: its subfolders and its files, each with a one-line description (authored summary, else derived) and a section index (stable anchors). Generalises get_doc_tree one level up. Serves the corpus 'what do we know' table of contents (e.g. over 'Chat conversations/'). Pass an empty folder_id for the drive root.

folder_id.description: Folder to expand; empty for the drive root.

tools.name: get_memory
tools.title: Get memory
tools.description: Return the tenant's Memory/ for inlining at conversation start. mode 'full' (whole bodies) under the size budget, else mode 'tree' (one-line summary per memory) with lazy drill-down via read_file. Guaranteed enumeration: never search-only.

tools.name: write_memory
tools.title: Write a memory
tools.description: Write or update one standing fact in Memory/ (one fact per file, kebab-case name, authored summary). Returns overlapping memories (merge hygiene: update in place rather than duplicate). Set overwrite to replace an existing name; set by_assistant for assistant-authored writes (raises a wallet notification). Body may use [[wikilinks]] to other memories.

summary.description: One-line hook, stored as frontmatter summary.

overwrite.description: Replace an existing memory of this name.

tools.name: rearm_tenant_key
tools.title: Re-arm tenant vault key
tools.description: Refresh the attestation token on the caller's personal-tenant vault MEK and reload it. Run this after a 409 vault_key_stale (the stored attestation token expires about 15 minutes after the last re-arm; the wallet re-arms on login). Pass a fresh aud=attestation-server token.

attestation_token.description: A fresh aud=attestation-server bearer used by the enclave to verify the vaults' quotes when reading key shares back.

tools.name: set_bucket_cred
tools.title: Set BYO bucket credential
tools.description: Store (or rotate) the tenant's bring-your-own cloud bucket credential, sealed under a vault wrapped-secret key whose policy grants this app's enclave Unwrap only. The plaintext credential never appears here: the body carries the vault-sealed ciphertext plus the operator key reference. Once set, the tenant's chunks are stored in their own bucket (still opaque AEAD ciphertext). Owner/admin of the tenant only.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the credential belongs to.

key_ref.description: The wrapped-secret operator key reference: {handle, endpoints, mrenclave, attestation_server, attestation_token, threshold} as printed by 'privasys vault key create --kind wrapped-secret'.

ciphertext_b64.description: The sealed credential ciphertext (raw-url base64, no padding).

iv_b64.description: The AES-GCM IV from the vault Wrap (raw-url base64, no padding).

bucket.description: Target bucket name (plaintext metadata; the sealed blob holds only the credential material).

tools.name: get_bucket_cred
tools.title: Get BYO bucket credential status
tools.description: Report whether the tenant has a BYO bucket credential configured, plus its non-secret metadata (content type, bucket, key handle). Never returns the ciphertext or plaintext.

tenant_id.description: The tenant to inspect.

tools.name: provision_org_mek
tools.title: Provision the org master key (escrowed mode)
tools.description: Create MEK_org for an escrowed instance from a key-creation grant bundle fetched from the control plane. The grant is the authorisation (IdP-signed, bound to this app, owned by the org admin); the enclave generates the key and Shamir-splits it across the vault constellation. Returns the org_mek_ref JSON to set via the configure tool. The key material never leaves confidential hardware.

handle.description: The key handle from the grant response (handle_hint).

tools.name: request_recovery
tools.title: Request tenant recovery (escrowed mode)
tools.description: File an escrowed-mode recovery request for a tenant. Only a policy-permitted requester may file one; it grants nothing by itself. The configured approver quorum must then approve (approve_recovery); at quorum the enclave unwraps the tenant key from its escrow wrap under MEK_org and mints a TIME-BOUNDED tenant-wide read grant for the grantee. Every step is audited and disclosed to the affected tenant.

tenant_id.description: The tenant to recover.

reason.description: Why this recovery is needed (audited, disclosed to the tenant).

grantee_sub.description: The subject who receives the time-bounded read grant.

ttl_seconds.description: Read-grant lifetime in seconds (default 86400, max 7 days).

tools.name: approve_recovery
tools.title: Approve a pending recovery
tools.description: Deliver one approver's approval for a pending recovery. On privasys.id the token must come from the operation-bound WebAuthn ceremony (the approver's wallet signs the recovery digest); on an org IdP a fresh bearer from the configured issuer is accepted. One approval per approver, single-use tokens. Executes automatically when the quorum is reached.

tenant_id.description: The tenant under recovery.

approval_token.description: The approver's token (ceremony token on privasys.id; fresh issuer bearer otherwise).

tools.name: recovery_status
tools.title: Recovery status
tools.description: Report a recovery's progress: status, approvals vs quorum, and the ceremony parameters (handle + digest) an approver needs to run the WebAuthn approval.

tenant_id.description: The tenant under recovery.

tools.name: delete_bucket_cred
tools.title: Remove BYO bucket credential
tools.description: Clear the tenant's BYO bucket credential; new chunks fall back to the platform-managed bucket. Owner/admin of the tenant only.

tenant_id.description: The tenant whose credential to remove.

tools.name: purge_tenant
tools.title: Purge an unreachable tenant
tools.description: Permanently remove a tenant: sealed chunks (best effort), then the index (folders, files, members, grants and pending link requests). For tenants nobody can unlock any more, typically after an identity rotation on a sovereign instance, or for offboarding. Instance operator (app owner/admin) only; the purge is recorded in the append-only audit log, which survives the tenant. Irreversible.

tenant_id.description: The tenant to purge.

reason.description: Why the tenant is being purged (audited).

tools.name: search_semantic
tools.title: Semantic search (agentic RAG)
tools.description: Search a tenant's drive semantically over the in-enclave embeddings index. Every hit carries provenance: node_id, section_id, section_path (title chain) and char range, so a consumer can cite which files and which parts of them an answer used. Follow up with get_doc_tree to see a file's structure, read_section for a whole section, or read_file for the full content.

tenant_id.description: The tenant whose drive to search.

assistant_scope.description: Restrict results to the AI-scoped node set (Memory/, Chat conversations/, and directories enabled for AI). Enforced server-side.

tools.name: get_doc_tree
tools.title: Document structure (table of contents)
tools.description: Return a file's section tree: titles, depth, char (and page, when known) ranges, and section summaries when present. The agent's table of contents for hierarchical retrieval: navigate the tree, then call read_section on the relevant entries.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the file belongs to.

tools.name: read_section
tools.title: Read one document section
tools.description: Return one whole section's text with provenance (title, char and page range). Whole sections beat fragments as answer context; use after get_doc_tree or a search_semantic hit.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the file belongs to.

section_id.description: The section id from get_doc_tree or a search hit.
